decision: The board remains complete locally and release no-go. Local source checks pass, but Mitra is not release-ready or growth-ready until P0/P1 live, provider, Figma Make, platform, and owner blockers are remediated and verified.

localScanFindings.title: Local build and deterministic source checks pass
localScanFindings.evidence: npm run build, i18n:audit, sitemap:check, feed:check, and commerce:check passed.
localScanFindings.impact: The remaining blocker pattern is not a broad local compile/source failure.

localScanFindings.title: Vite build copies SEO static assets into the release output
localScanFindings.evidence: vite.config.ts uses publicDir src/public and outDir build; local build produced robots.txt, sitemap.xml, product sitemaps, merchant-products.xml, _headers, and _redirects in build.
localScanFindings.impact: Production asset mismatch is likely deployment target, cache, hosting, or Figma Make/provider routing, not missing local files.

localScanFindings.title: CMS route is still a public SPA route at the source/edge contract level
localScanFindings.evidence: src/public/_redirects contains /cms / 200 and /cms/* / 200, while src/public/robots.txt only disallows crawling. SiteApp routes /cms, /admin aliases, and /pwa into client-rendered protected screens.
localScanFindings.impact: Robots/noindex cannot protect private content. The route needs edge/app authorization before any private interface can render.

localScanFindings.title: Checkout URL/canonical bug has a direct local source owner
localScanFindings.evidence: CartDrawer onCheckout only calls setCurrentPage('checkout') from SiteApp, without navigate('/checkout').
localScanFindings.impact: Checkout can render while the browser URL remains a product URL, corrupting product SEO state and user trust.

localScanFindings.title: Legacy/product-ID redirect behavior needs a real HTTP layer
localScanFindings.evidence: Client-side product identifier canonicalization uses history.replaceState after loading the item. HTTP requests still depend on SPA fallback unless an edge redirect layer handles old IDs and legacy route aliases.
localScanFindings.impact: Search engines and crawlers can see HTTP 200 soft states instead of durable one-hop 301 redirects.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Public/private boundary and CMS route protection
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: This is the highest-risk blocker because public/private boundary failures stop release regardless of SEO improvements.
acceptance: Unauthenticated /cms, /cms/*, /admin/*, /pwa/*, account/private routes return 401, 403, or safe 404 before private content renders.
No private records or staff UI render to unauthenticated browser sessions.
Robots.txt is retained only as crawler guidance, not as security.
Any confirmed exposed private data has owner cleanup/rotation decision.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Figma Make source sync and preview verification
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: The production/Figma surface can reintroduce stale runtime errors even though local source builds.
acceptance: Figma Make is patched from the E-1 list.
Preview no longer throws CONTACT_INFO or stale import/runtime errors.
Only Figma Make source files are listed in Figma sync closeouts.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Production static SEO assets and Merchant feed deployment parity
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: Local build includes the assets, but production evidence shows robots/sitemap/feed mismatches.
acceptance: Production robots.txt returns 200 text/plain.
Production sitemap.xml and product sitemaps return 200 XML, not the SPA shell.
Production merchant-products.xml returns 200 XML and is fetchable by Merchant Center.
Search Console and Merchant Center readback confirm submitted/fetched assets.

nextWorkBacklog.name: HTTP redirect, product ID migration, and soft-404 remediation
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: Client-side route correction is not enough for migration, crawl, or canonical policy.
implementationFiles: new provider edge function or Worker for dynamic product ID-to-slug redirects
acceptance: Legacy service/category samples 301 one hop to canonical URLs.
Product UUID/SKU/code URLs 301 one hop to canonical slug URLs.
Unknown routes return real 404 or 410 with safe noindex content.
Redirect checks run before the SPA fallback in deployed provider behavior.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Checkout URL, canonical, and deployed Paytrail parity
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: Product SEO and commerce trust require URL, cart, checkout, stock, price, and payment state to agree.
acceptance: Cart checkout action navigates to /checkout.
Checkout remains noindex and cannot corrupt product canonical state.
Deployed payments_create_paytrail revalidates product identity, price, availability, and stock from authoritative data.
Browser smoke verifies product add-to-cart, checkout start, Paytrail handoff path, and cancel/success recovery.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Authenticated provider and platform readback
nextWorkBacklog.owner: Provider, SEO, analytics, local, merchant, and business owners
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: Unavailable platform evidence cannot be counted as pass or growth readiness.
acceptance: Cloudflare account/zone/project/routes/headers/redirects/deployment state are read back without secrets.
Search Console ownership, sitemap submission, URL inspections, manual actions, security issues, enhancements, and performance are captured.
GBP, Merchant Center, analytics, logs, and field CWV evidence are captured or explicitly owner-excepted.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Business/local/content owner evidence package
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: Local/service/product content can be structurally improved but still not growth-ready without owner-approved facts.
acceptance: GBP ownership, categories, hours, service area, website URL, booking URL, reviews process, and citation facts are owner-approved.
Service prices, durations, constraints, safety claims, warranties, and local proof are approved or omitted.
No invented claims, reviews, credentials, or unsupported comparison language are published.

nextWorkBacklog.name: Post-remediation live crawl, browser smoke, and drift rerun
nextWorkBacklog.whyNow: The release decision can only reopen after fixes are verified on the deployed site.
acceptance: Rerun representative live HTTP checks.
Rerun browser smoke for homepage, services, product, cart, checkout, contact, invalid route, and protected routes.
Compare against E-4 drift baseline and record deltas.
Reopen Gate D/Gate E only after acceptance evidence is attached.

cleanup: build removed after local build verification
noGuarantee: This scan prioritizes implementation, release, and growth-readiness work. It does not guarantee crawling, indexing, selected canonicals, rankings, rich results, local-pack visibility, Merchant Center approval, traffic, conversions, revenue, or AI inclusion.
